title: Jugurtha
content:
How cold are thy baths, Apollo!
        Cried the African monarch, the splendid,
    As down to his death in the hollow
        Dark dungeons of Rome he descended,
        Uncrowned, unthroned, unattended;
    How cold are thy baths, Apollo!

    How cold are thy baths, Apollo!
        Cried the Poet, unknown, unbefriended,
    As the vision, that lured him to follow,
        With the mist and the darkness blended,
        And the dream of his life was ended;
    How cold are thy baths, Apollo!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Henry Wadsworth Longfellow delivers a powerful performance in "Jugurtha"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
